fields.description:
## Summary
set-file-impact / set-verify-commands accept arbitrary array elements — only array-ness is validated, so junk violating the documented element contract is stored and flows downstream.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. rebar set-file-impact <id> '[42, "string"]' -> exit 0; get-file-impact -> [42, "string"]
2. rebar set-file-impact <id> '[{"reason":"no path"}]' -> exit 0
3. rebar set-verify-commands <id> '[{"x":1}]' -> exit 0
4. Control: non-JSON and non-array inputs ARE rejected.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected (help text): "JSON array of {path,reason} objects" / "{dd_id,dd_text,command}".
- Actual: any array contents accepted; junk reaches show/list/--output llm payloads and (once bug vamp-floor-mica is fixed) the scheduler's conflict detection.

## Source
src/rebar/_engine/ticket-lib-api.sh:1027-1036 (file_impact), :1194-1201 (verify_commands) — validation stops at jq type == array.

## Fix direction
Per-element jq validation: every element an object containing the required string keys; reject with exit 1 naming the first offending index. Preserve current empty-array semantics. Validated jq expression + [] behavior in the implementation-notes comment. Sequence with/after vamp-floor-mica (relates_to link exists).

## Suggested RED test
Each malformed shape above -> exit 1 with index in message; valid arrays and [] -> accepted; round-trip via get-* unchanged for valid input.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Element-level validation on both setters (RED tests per malformed shape first)
- [ ] Error names the offending index and the required keys
- [ ] [] semantics preserved; valid-input round-trip unchanged
- [ ] MCP set_file_impact/set_verify_commands surface the same rejection cleanly
